channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Approval of Texas' Bible-infused curriculum will likely depend on Abbott appointee
item.description: Tiffany Clark, who was elected to fill a vacant State Board of Education seat in January, said she would have voted against the curriculum.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Jaden Edison, KPRC 2

item.title: RFK Jr's abortion 'issue': Senate GOP plans to scrutinize Trump HHS pick's position
item.description: Republican senators have some concerns about Trump's choice of Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. to lead the Department of Health and Human Services.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers are concerned about background checks of Trump's Cabinet picks as red flags surface
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As senators prepare to consider President-elect Donald Trump's picks for his Cabinet, they may be doing so without a well-established staple of the confirmation process: an FBI background check.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ZEKE MILLER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about Matthew Whitaker, Trump’s pick to be America's ambassador to NATO
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump has tapped former acting attorney general Matthew Whitaker as U.S. ambassador to NATO — set to make him the nation's representative to a bedrock Western alliance that the president-elect has repeatedly relished criticizing.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The US is sending antipersonnel land mines to Ukraine. Here's what it means
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. decision to provide Ukraine with antipersonnel land mines expands the use of a weapon that the international community has long condemned because of its danger to innocent civilians.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Republicans on House Ethics reject for now releasing report on Matt Gaetz
item.description: Republicans on the House Ethics Committee voted Wednesday against releasing a report on their long-running investigation into President-elect Donald Trump’s nominee for attorney general, former Rep. Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Lisa Mascaro And Stephen Groves, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump taps former acting AG Matthew Whitaker as NATO ambassador
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced former Acting Attorney General Matthew Whitaker as his choice for U.S. ambassador to NATO.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans on Ethics Committee weigh 'unenviable' Gaetz report decision
item.description: Republicans on the House Ethics Committee are barreling into a no-win situation ahead of their expected meeting Wednesday to discuss their report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), weighing a high-stakes decision that will reverberate throughout Washington.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump Cabinet picks give some of his Muslim voters pause
item.description: Muslims for Trump co-founder Rabiul Chowdhury said some of the nominations are concerning for voters.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to know about Linda McMahon, Trump's pick for education secretary
item.description: If confirmed by the Senate, McMahon will be put in charge of heading up changes to Title IX and determining how student debt is handled.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Few Americans use 'woke' terminology: Survey
item.description: In the YouGov survey, under a quarter of Americans polled said they “regularly use” words and phrases such as “safe space,” “woke,” "lived experience” and “white privilege.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Republicans fume as GOP absences help Democrats move judicial nominees
item.description: Senate Republicans aired frustrations Tuesday after Vice President-elect JD Vance and other party members skipped votes Monday, greasing the skids for Democratic-backed judicial nominees to be greenlighted as part of a final push to fill the bench with lifetime appointees before President-elect Trum
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to nominate Mehmet Oz for CMS administrator
item.description: President-elect Trump said Tuesday he is nominating television personality Dr. Mehmet Oz to serve as the Centers for Medicare and Medicaid Services (CMS) administrator. Oz unsuccessfully ran for Senate in Pennsylvania in 2022 with Trump's endorsement and has been a Trump ally ever since.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Senate report claims Biden failures in protecting migrant children
item.description: GOP Sen. Bill Cassidy launched a formal investigation more than a year ago into unaccompanied migrant kids.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's hush money case should be paused, not dismissed: Prosecutors
item.description: Trump was convicted in May of falsifying business records to cover up a $130,000 payment his former lawyer Michael Cohen made to porn star Stormy Daniels.
item.dc:creator: Luc Cohen / Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: When Trump left office, students were struggling because of the pandemic. They still are
item.description: When President-elect Donald Trump left office in early 2021, the pandemic was at its height. Now he returns as American children and schools are struggling to overcome its lingering impact.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA chief agrees to investigation after congressional hearing
item.description: Questions have focused on the agency’s preparedness and response to recent disasters, including Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson becomes 'MAGA' Mike, cementing place in Trump's circle
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is cementing his place in President-elect Trump’s inner orbit, becoming not only a visible — if slightly out-of-place-looking — part of his entourage, but a key supporter of his no-holds-barred approach to his second term.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, WGN 9

item.title: Pennsylvania's high court orders counties not to count disputed ballots in US Senate race
item.description: The order is a win for McCormick and a loss for Casey as the campaigns prepare for a statewide recount and press counties for favorable ballot-counting decisions while election workers are sorting through thousands of provisional ballots.
item.dc:creator: MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane debate at state capitol heats up as temperatures cool down
item.description: Cooler temperatures mean hurricane season is almost behind us, but the debate in the state capital over storm recovery is just heating up.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Defense pick Peter Hegseth paid accuser to protect his career, his lawyer confirms
item.description: The Fox News host reached a settlement to resolve an accusation against him, but a recently released memo brought the issue back into the public eye.
item.dc:creator: Bill Chappell, MPR News

item.title: Trump signals use of military for deportations
item.description: President-elect Trump recirculated a social media post Monday suggesting his incoming administration will declare an immigration national emergency and use military assets to support his mass deportation pledge, labeling the claim “TRUE!!!” On his Truth Social site, Trump at 4:03 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Rafael Bernal, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking break from Cabinet picks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Some in the U.S. farm industry are alarmed by Trump's embrace of RFK Jr. and tariffs
item.description: President-elect Trump won landslide support in much of farm country, but his embrace of Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and his plan for a tariff fight with China alarms many farmers and agriculture experts.
item.dc:creator: Brian Mann, MPR News

item.title: 1 in 5 US residents 'more likely' to move after election, according to Redfin
item.description: Of those who say they are now more likely to move, 36% say the election results are making them consider a different country.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump is creating team of loyalists after conflicts with Cabinet members in 1st term
item.description: In his second term, President-elect Donald Trump is surrounding himself with people who can amplify his message and agree with how he wants to remake the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trump names Karoline Leavitt as White House press secretary
item.description: Leavitt, 27, previously served in the Trump White House as assistant press secretary under Kayleigh McEnany, now a leading host on Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump spokesperson Steven Cheung to serve as White House communications director
item.description: Steven Cheung, who has served as a top press aide on the Trump campaign for the past two years, will join the incoming administration as White House communications director, President-elect Trump announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5

item.title: The last actions the Biden administration will take before Trump takes over the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Biden administration officials are working against the clock doling out billions in grants and taking other steps to try to preserve at least some of the outgoing president’s legacy before President-elect Donald Trump takes office in January.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, MATTHEW DALY and COLLIN BINKLEY Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Abbott to Texas universities: Freeze tuition
item.description: Texas Gov. Greg Abbott (R) announced Wednesday that he has sent letters to all public universities in the state saying he would not support tuition raises over the next two years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5
